canonical_intent: Test whether governed execution lets humans delegate desired software outcomes with less procedural micromanagement and ongoing intervention while preserving outcome quality, intent fidelity, recoverability, and reviewability.

concurrent_fleet.label: Study C
concurrent_fleet.question: Can independently launched agents concurrently solve similar-but-distinct problems in one codebase while preserving authority, custody, dependency awareness, and integrated proof?

walk_away_rule: Start one complex natural delegation prompt, then provide no corrective coaching; clarification uses only a frozen oracle.
pre_freeze_evolution: Experiments, hypotheses, conditions, instrumentation, and sample plans may change as Decapod develops; every revision must be versioned and justified. After freeze, material changes require a new protocol version or a disclosed deviation.
synthetic_fixture_boundary: Legacy three-condition files and aggregates remain synthetic_fixture and cannot be aggregated with pilot or empirical records.
study_boundary: Aggregation must select exactly one study_id; longitudinal observational records cannot enter controlled summaries and one study's metrics cannot enter another study's headline result.
cloud_boundary: The inspected public Decapod crate is local-first; cloud backend execution is unavailable and remote federation is future work.
